channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Biden makes rare dip into battleground state fray with visits to Pennsylvania and Wisconsin
item.description: BRYN MAWR, Penn. (AP) — President Joe Biden made a rare jump into the 2024 political battleground fray since taking a step back after ending his reelection bid. He campaigned Tuesday in Pennsylvania for a close ally after having stopped in Wisconsin to spotlight a signature legislative achievement.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Texas authorities arrest dozens of illegal immigrants, including 25 unaccompanied children
item.description: Texas state troopers on Wednesday arrested more than 100 illegal immigrants near the U.S.-Mexico border, 25 of whom were unaccompanied children.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A retired Houston officer gets 60 years in a couple's drug raid deaths that revealed corruption
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A former Houston police officer was sentenced to 60 years in prison on Tuesday for the murder of a married couple during a drug raid that revealed systemic corruption in the department’s narcotics unit and prompted criticism of the law enforcement agency and its tactics.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia university leaders ask NCAA to ban transgender women from sports
item.description: The regents who govern Georgia’s public universities and colleges want the NCAA to ban transgender women from participating in women’s sports.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Amy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Beware of Hurricane Helene relief scams, officials warn
item.description: In the wake of Hurricane Helene, thousands of people have responded to assist with recovery efforts — but officials are now warning of hurricane-related fraud schemes.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: How will Hurricane Milton stack up against other major recent storms?
item.description: As a ferocious Hurricane Milton charges its way across the Gulf Coast with Tampa and St. Petersburg in its sights, thousands flee the area for safer ground, leaving behind debris from Hurricane Helene's destruction less that two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: VIDEO: Hurricane hunters take turbulent trip through Milton
item.description: Researchers on a hurricane hunter aircraft had a bumpy ride as they flew into Hurricane Milton on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, KRON4

item.title: Why do people fill their bathtubs with water before a hurricane?
item.description: If you live in a hurricane-prone area, it may be common practice for you. If you live well outside that region, you may be confused by the move.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Muggers ripped watch off Dodgers pitcher Walker Buehler's arm, police say
item.description:
Police in California say thieves surrounded Los Angeles Dodgers starting pitcher Walker Buehler last month and ripped a pricey watch off his arm.
Police say the victims were all targets of organized groups who steal high-end watches in large crowds during events.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Airline apologizes after playing explicit movie on every screen
item.description: Australia's Qantas Airways is apologizing after showing a movie that contains scenes of nudity, graphic language and sexting to the passengers aboard a recent flight from Sydney to Haneda, Japan.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Conservative senator lays out proposals for post-McConnell reforms
item.description: Sen. Mike Lee (R-Utah), the chair of the conservative Senate Steering Committee, has laid out a list of proposed reforms designed to ensure that the next Senate Republican leader does not have the same power as outgoing Senate GOP Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.).
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: October Prime Day has a ton of emergency supply deals
item.description: Everyone should have supplies on hand to ride out an emergency. And thanks to Amazon's October Prime Day, you can create a kit without breaking the bank.
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's shifting abortion stance raises questions for evangelical support
item.description: Former President Trump’s softening stance on reproductive health has raised questions about his standing with white evangelical voters, a key part of his coalition dating back to 2016.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Shots fired after victim, friends track stolen truck in Memphis
item.description: "We were waiting on another friend to come, and he actually saw them pushing my truck out of the lot," the victim said.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Moon, NewsNation

item.title: Is it safe to be outside in Hurricane Milton's eye? Here's what experts say
item.description: The eye of a hurricane is usually calm compared to the storm swirling around it — but is it safe to be outside as the eye passes over you?
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KRON4

item.title: Could a drug used in organ transplants slow aging?
item.description: Studies so far suggest rapamycin and its derivatives can have positive effects on the immune, cardiovascular, and other systems.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: DeSantis pushes back on 'delusional' Harris remark about Hurricane Milton calls
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) said Monday that it was “delusional” for Vice President Kamala Harris to accuse him of “political gamesmanship” after reports emerged that he did not take the Democratic presidential nominee’s calls as his state braces for Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump campaign blasts Bob Woodward; denies secret Putin calls
item.description: Donald Trump's campaign is rejecting claims in Bob Woodward's new book 'War' about the former president holding secret calls with Vladimir Putin since leaving office.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KRON4

item.title: Texas woman robbed, pistol-whipped in ritzy Dallas area by illegal Venezuelan migrants with gang ties: report
item.description: Four Venezuelan illegal immigrants have been arrested and charged with robbing a woman at gunpoint in the driveway of her home in Dallas' Bluffview neighborhood.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Melania Trump waves off 'hand-swatting incident': A 'mere misunderstanding'
item.description: Melania Trump is waving off speculation that she didn't want to hold her husband's hand, chalking up images of her seemingly "swatting" away former President Donald Trump to a "mere misunderstanding.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Canyoneer dies after accidental 150-foot fall at Zion National Park
item.description: A man fell while canyoneering at Zion National Park on Saturday, resulting in his death, according to the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Harris reveals owning a Glock: 'Of course' I've fired it
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris said in a "60 Minutes" interview that aired Monday that she owns a Glock and has fired it at a shooting range in another example of how she and her campaign have made a point to highlight her gun ownership.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Milton may be October surprise in presidential battle
item.description: Strategists in both parties are looking back to Hurricane Sandy, which slammed into the mid-Atlantic states shortly before Election Day 2012.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bizarre laws in Washington, such as being charged with reckless driving if hugging while behind the wheel
item.description: Among Washington's strangest laws include penalties for using X-rays as shoe-fitting devices as well as trouble for intimidating another with a laser.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What polling shows about Black voters' views of Harris and Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Black registered voters have an overwhelmingly positive view of Vice President Kamala Harris, but they’re less sure that she would change the country for the better, according to a recent poll from the AP-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why is Tampa Bay so vulnerable to hurricanes?
item.description: As Milton barrels toward the Gulf Coast of Florida, experts are worried that a century of luck could come to a sudden end.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Milton again becomes Category 5 hurricane as residents flee Tampa Bay region
item.description: Fearful Florida residents streamed out of the Tampa Bay region Tuesday ahead of what could be a once-in-a-century direct hit from Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Hurricane Milton is a Category 5. Florida orders evacuations and scrambles to clear Helene's debris
item.description: BELLEAIR BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Florida’s storm-battered Gulf Coast raced against a Category 5 hurricane Monday as workers sprinted to pick up debris left over from Helene two weeks ago and highways were clogged with people fleeing ahead of the storm.
item.dc:creator: HAVEN DALEY and MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: When will we 'fall back?' What Utahns have tried to get daylight saving time permanent
item.description: It is almost time to fall back, the first weekend of November marks the end of daylight saving across much of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Mahon, ABC 4

item.title: James Taylor, Luke Combs, Eric Church to take part in Helene relief concert in NC
item.description: "Concert for Carolina," featuring North Carolina natives James Taylor, Luke Combs and Eric Church, will be held at Bank of America Stadium in Charlotte with all proceeds going to Hurricane Helene relief efforts across the Carolina region, organizers announced.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Ullmann, Fox 5

item.title: Live Wobble Tracker: Map shows real-time movements of Hurricane Milton
item.description: Hurricane Milton, which reached Category 5 strength Monday, is heading right for Florida's west coast, with a potential landfall near or at Tampa Bay.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, Fox 5

item.title: Judge rules the FTC can proceed with antitrust lawsuit against Amazon, tosses out few state claims
item.description: A federal judge said the Federal Trade Commission can proceed with its landmark antitrust lawsuit against Amazon. But, he also gave the company a small victory by tossing out a few claims made by states involved in the legal fight. The order, issued last week by Judge John H.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court won’t hear Pennsylvania legislators' challenge to executive actions expanding voting access
item.description: The Supreme Court decided Monday it won’t take up a case to restore a lawsuit by Pennsylvania Republicans that challenged executive actions expanding voting access on the basis that only state legislatures can regulate federal elections.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KRON4

item.title: Supreme Court takes the bench with ghost guns, a capital case and transgender rights on the docket
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court took the bench again on Monday, ready to hear cases on ghost guns, a death sentence and transgender rights.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Walmart will be closed on Thanksgiving Day for 5th consecutive year
item.description: Walmart will be closed on Thanksgiving Day for the fifth year in a row, the retail giant recently announced in its holiday plans.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk makes appearance at Trump rally casting election in dire terms
item.description: Billionaire tech executive Elon Musk cast the upcoming presidential election in dire terms during a Saturday appearance with Donald Trump, calling the Republican presidential nominee the only candidate “to preserve democracy in America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: When will the power return? Weary Carolinas residents long for relief after Helene's fury
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — The weary and worn residents of Julianne Johnson's neighborhood in Asheville have been getting by without electricity since Hurricane Helene tore through the Southeast last week and upended their lives.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, WGN 9
